prompt:
Analyze the image and generate an open-vocabulary scene graph.
Return:
1. A short scene description.
2. Objects with normalized bounding boxes in this format: <ref>object</ref><box>[[x1, y1, x2, y2]]</box>.
3. Spatial relations in this format: <pred>predicate</pred><box>[[subject_box]]</box><box>[[object_box]]</box>.
4. Depth layers from closest to farthest.
5. Four comparative question-answer pairs about object position, depth, or size.
Use coordinates from 0 to 999. Be concise.
answer:
1. A close-up of a white cat resting on blankets, with the cat’s central face obscured by a square blur.

2. Objects  
<ref>white cat</ref><box>[[0, 0, 824, 999]]</box>  
<ref>obscured face square</ref><box>[[168, 238, 730, 953]]</box>  
<ref>left ear</ref><box>[[98, 0, 324, 504]]</box>  
<ref>right ear</ref><box>[[625, 0, 820, 504]]</box>  
<ref>right-side whiskers</ref><box>[[699, 582, 996, 879]]</box>  
<ref>white cat fur</ref><box>[[0, 387, 664, 999]]</box>  
<ref>green blanket</ref><box>[[723, 566, 999, 999]]</box>  
<ref>beige blanket</ref><box>[[0, 0, 176, 516]]</box>  
<ref>dark background fabric</ref><box>[[246, 0, 629, 301]]</box>

3. Spatial relations  
<pred>covers</pred><box>[[168, 238, 730, 953]]</box><box>[[0, 0, 824, 999]]</box>  
<pred>above</pred><box>[[98, 0, 324, 504]]</box><box>[[168, 238, 730, 953]]</box>  
<pred>above</pred><box>[[625, 0, 820, 504]]</box><box>[[168, 238, 730, 953]]</box>  
<pred>left of</pred><box>[[98, 0, 324, 504]]</box><box>[[625, 0, 820, 504]]</box>  
<pred>right of</pred><box>[[699, 582, 996, 879]]</box><box>[[168, 238, 730, 953]]</box>  
<pred>behind</pred><box>[[723, 566, 999, 999]]</box><box>[[0, 0, 824, 999]]</box>  
<pred>behind</pred><box>[[0, 0, 176, 516]]</box><box>[[0, 0, 824, 999]]</box>  
<pred>behind</pred><box>[[246, 0, 629, 301]]</box><box>[[0, 0, 824, 999]]</box>

4. Depth layers  
- Closest: obscured face square  
- Near: cat head, ears, whiskers, white fur  
- Mid-background: green blanket, beige blanket  
- Farthest: dark background fabric

5. Comparative Q&A  
Q: Which ear is farther left?  
A: The left ear is farther left than the right ear.

Q: Which object is closest to the viewer?  
A: The obscured face square appears closest.

Q: Which is larger, the cat or the green blanket?  
A: The visible cat region is larger.

Q: Are the whiskers left or right of the obscured face square?  
A: The whiskers are to the right of the obscured face square.
